2603.17829.title: CodeScout: An Effective Recipe for Reinforcement Learning of Code Search Agents
2603.17829.paper_summary: A prerequisite for coding agents to perform tasks on large repositories is code localization - the identification of relevant files, classes, and functions to work on. While repository-level code localization has been performed using embedding-based retrieval approaches such as vector search, recent work has focused on developing agents to localize relevant code either as a standalone precursor to or interleaved with performing actual work. Most prior methods on agentic code search equip the agent with complex, specialized tools, such as repository graphs derived from static analysis. In this paper, we demonstrate that, with an effective reinforcement learning recipe, a coding agent equipped with nothing more than a standard Unix terminal can be trained to achieve strong results. Our experiments on three benchmarks (SWE-Bench Verified, Pro, and Lite) reveal that our models consistently achieve superior or competitive performance over 2-18x larger base and post-trained LLMs and sometimes approach performance provided by closed models like Claude Sonnet, even when using specialized scaffolds. Our work particularly focuses on techniques for re-purposing existing coding agent environments for code search, reward design, and RL optimization. We release the resulting model family, CodeScout, along with all our code and data for the community to build upon.
2603.17829.paper_summary_zh: 一個編碼代理在大型代碼庫上執行任務的前提是代碼定位——識別相關的文件、類別和函數以進行操作。雖然已經使用基於嵌入的檢索方法（例如向量搜索）進行了庫級代碼定位，但最近的研究集中在開發代理，以便將相關代碼定位為獨立的前驅或與實際工作交錯進行。大多數先前的代理代碼搜索方法為代理配備了複雜的專用工具，例如來自靜態分析的庫圖。本文中，我們展示了通過有效的強化學習配方，僅用標準Unix終端裝備的編碼代理可以被訓練以達到優異的結果。我們在三個基準（SWE-Bench Verified、Pro和Lite）上的實驗顯示，我們的模型在性能上始終優於或與2-18倍更大的基礎和後訓練LLM競爭，有時甚至接近像Claude Sonnet這樣的封閉模型提供的性能，即使在使用專用支架的情況下。我們的工作特別關注於重新利用現有編碼代理環境進行代碼搜索、獎勵設計和強化學習優化的技術。我們發布了結果模型系列CodeScout，以及我們的所有代碼和數據，供社區進一步構建。
2603.17829.author: Lintang Sutawika et.al.
2603.17829.authors: Lintang Sutawika, Aditya Bharat Soni, Bharath Sriraam R R, Apurva Gandhi, Taha Yassine, Sanidhya Vijayvargiya, Yuchen Li, Xuhui Zhou, Yilin Zhang, Leander Melroy Maben, Graham Neubig
